---
title: pelican cache purge
---

## pelican cache purge

Trigger LRU eviction on the running cache

### Synopsis

Trigger an immediate LRU eviction cycle on the running cache.

Without --target, evicts down to the configured low water mark.
With --target, evicts down to the specified total cache size.

The --target value accepts human-readable sizes: 100GB, 500MB, 1TB, etc.

Examples:
  pelican cache purge
  pelican cache purge --target 100GB
  pelican cache purge --token /path/to/token

```
pelican cache purge [flags]
```

### Options

```
  -h, --help            help for purge
      --target string   Target cache size (e.g. 100GB, 500MB); default: configured low water mark
  -t, --token string    Path to token file (auto-generated if not provided)
```

### Options inherited from parent commands

```
      --config string       config file (default is $HOME/.config/pelican/pelican.yaml)
  -d, --debug               Enable debug log messages
  -f, --federation string   Pelican federation to utilize
      --json                output results in JSON format
  -L, --log string          Specified log output file
      --version             Print the version and exit
```
